messages.content:
CVE-2024-57887 addresses a use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel's DRM subsystem specifically related to the adv7511 module. The issue arises within the adv7533_attach_dsi() function where memory is referenced after being freed, leading to potential system instability or exploitation. This vulnerability is classified under CWE-416 and is considered to have a high severity due to its capacity to cause system crashes or unintended code execution.

The specific MITRE ATT&CK technique associated with this vulnerability is T1068. The affected versions include several releases of the Linux kernel, from version 4.8 up to 6.12.9, as well as specific release candidates of version 6.13. To mitigate this issue, it is recommended to update to the latest patched version as fixes have been implemented to prevent exploitation by correcting the memory handling procedures involved in the process.

Organizations should apply mitigation techniques M1051 (Update Software), M1050 (Data Execution Prevention), M1048 (Execution Prevention), M1019 (User Training), and M1038 (Secure Configuration) as additional security measures to further protect systems from these types of vulnerabilities.
